mechanism: Selective BCL-2 inhibition, promotes apoptosis in BCL-2–dependent cells
additional_notes: Approved; often low- to mid-nM IC50 in AML (MOLM13). DMSO soluble.

mechanism: Small-molecule FLT3 kinase inhibitor (targets FLT3-ITD)
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3-mutant AML; very potent (expected IC50 ~1–20 nM). DMSO soluble.

mechanism: Potent FLT3-ITD kinase inhibition
additional_notes: Highly potent against FLT3-ITD models; monitor solubility at high concentrations.

mechanism: Broad kinase inhibition including FLT3
additional_notes: Approved in AML settings; DMSO soluble.

mechanism: Combined BCL-2 inhibition and FLT3 kinase inhibition — expected synergistic apoptosis induction
additional_notes: Test single agents as above and fixed-ratio or matrix combos (example ratio venetoclax 100:1 gilteritinib). Evaluate synergy metrics (Bliss/ZIP).

mechanism: Inhibits BET bromodomains (BRD2/3/4), downregulates MYC and oncogenic transcription
additional_notes: Tool compound; transient transcriptional effects—48–72 h readout recommended. DMSO soluble.

mechanism: BET bromodomain inhibition (similar to JQ1), suppresses oncogenic transcription
additional_notes: Also known as MK-8628; clinical-stage BET inhibitor. DMSO soluble.

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs (notably CDK9), suppresses transcription of short-lived anti-apoptotic proteins
additional_notes: Also called flavopiridol; very potent—consider shorter exposures for some readouts.

mechanism: Reversible proteasome inhibition, disrupts protein homeostasis and induces apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved (hematologic malignancies); often tested with pulsed exposures. Water/formulation soluble.

mechanism: Inhibits HSP90, leading to degradation of client oncogenic kinases (e.g., FLT3)
additional_notes: Also called tanespimycin; monitor for heat-shock/stress response. DMSO soluble.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increases oxidative stress and induces cell death
additional_notes: Reported AML activity; oxidative stress assays recommended alongside viability readout. DMSO soluble.

mechanism: Pleiotropic: disrupts WNT/STAT/mTOR signaling and mitochondrial function leading to cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Poor aqueous solubility—use concentrated DMSO stocks and avoid precipitation.

mechanism: Forms copper complexes that inhibit proteasome/ALDH and induce apoptosis/oxidative stress
additional_notes: Requires copper for maximal activity (recommend test with low µM CuCl2, e.g., disulfiram 10 µM + 1 µM CuCl2; test ± copper).

mechanism: Disrupts microtubules and may affect other cancer-related pathways, causing cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Limited aqueous solubility—use DMSO stocks. Reported in vitro AML activity.

mechanism: Generates reactive oxygen species (ROS) and induces apoptosis / DNA damage
additional_notes: Also referenced as dihydroartemisinin; suggested top conc 50 µM (100 µM optional). Short half-life—monitor kinetics.

mechanism: Inhibits mevalonate pathway, affecting prenylation and can induce apoptosis/sensitization
additional_notes: Lipophilic prodrug—active hydroxy acid form may be required in vitro; consider pravastatin for hydrophilic comparisons.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism, activates AMPK, causes metabolic stress (often requires high concentrations in vitro)
additional_notes: Top conc 50 mM with 2-fold 10-point dilution (bottom ≈0.0977 mM). Reported IC50 in vitro often in 1–20 mM range—interpret cautiously vs clinical exposure.

mechanism: Multiple off-target effects (Hedgehog pathway inhibition, cholesterol trafficking, anti-angiogenic effects)
additional_notes: Lipophilic—prepare DMSO stocks. Reported anticancer activity in some models.

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH, blocks autophagy and lysosomal function, can sensitize cells to apoptosis
additional_notes: Also includes chloroquine as related agent; top conc 100 µM recommended. High concentrations may be cytostatic—interpret autophagy assays in parallel.

mechanism: Accumulates in lysosomes, induces ROS and apoptosis in cancer cell models
additional_notes: Lipophilic—use DMSO stocks; long intracellular retention may affect time-course and washout experiments.
